summary: Written the day after the September 11, 2001 attacks, this brief, poignant entry captures a moment of stillness in New York City. Spolsky observes the eerie beauty of perfect weather contrasted with the dust cloud still visible in the southern sky. He notes an elderly couple standing on a corner, holding hands and looking skyward, simply grateful to be alive. The post says everything by saying almost nothing.
key_points: The contrast between perfect weather and catastrophic destruction creates an unsettling dissonance
Small human gestures — holding hands, standing still — become profound in the aftermath of tragedy
The brevity itself is the message: some events render commentary inadequate
The dust in the southern sky serves as both literal observation and metaphor for what was lost
money_quotes: The southern sky is still filled with white dust.
They weren't going anywhere, just standing outside looking at the sky, happy to be here.
key_insight: In the face of catastrophe, the most powerful writing is simple witness — observing the dust in the sky and an old couple holding hands says more than any analysis could.
